preamble: The transformation of the voiceless bilabial plosive /p/ to the alveolar trill /r/ is an attested sound change, notably observed in the historical phonology of Yawalapiti, an Arawakan language of Brazil. This shift is understood to have originated from a process of contextual allophonic palatalization in a pre-Yawalapiti stage.

examples.note: This alternation is observed in the root for 'house' when possessed by 1st person singular (nu-piɲa 'my house') versus 2nd person singular (hi-riɲa 'your house'), indicating a palatalizing environment triggered by the 2nd person prefix.

sources: Carvalho, F. O. de. (2016). Internal and Comparative Reconstruction in Yawalapiti: Palatalization and Rule Telescoping. International Journal of American Linguistics, 82(3), 285–316.
